mnemonics: Matrix Multiplication = Matrix x Matrix, not Matrix x Vector
Matrix Determinant = det() or adply() for data frames
Matrix Inverse = inverse() or solve() for matrices
keyPoints: In R, the c() function is used to create a vector from a list of values
The length of a vector is calculated using nrow() or ncol() functions
The dim() function is used to get the dimensions of a matrix or data frame
The names() function is used to give names to variables in R
The format() function is used to convert a date or time object into a string
The as.Date() function is used to convert a character string into a date object
In R, the [ ] notation is used to access elements of a vector, matrix or data frame
The str() function is used to display the structure of a vector, matrix or data frame
The summary() function is used to display the summary statistics of a vector or data frame
